summary: First backend test iteration for AINTRIX Global API. All 34 pytest cases pass covering health, public reads (jobs/articles/research/article-by-slug), public writes (contacts/investor-leads/careers/internships), file upload (PDF accept + non-PDF/DOC reject), investor deck PDF generation, auth (login success/fail, /auth/me with & without token), admin stats + list endpoints (all 6), admin article full CRUD w/ duplicate-slug guard, admin job create/delete, and auth boundary checks on every /api/admin/* route.

minor.issue: backend/.env sets CORS_ORIGINS='*' and FRONTEND_URL='*' but server.py hardcodes allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False. Env values are unused. Non-blocking now but should be wired for future cookie-based auth.
Extension check only — no MIME sniffing or file-size cap. Acceptable for MVP but consider magic-byte validation + max size.

Review request listed slug 'aintrix-craft' which does not exist; actual seeded slug is 'aintrix-manifesto-century-of-compound-innovation'. Tested against the real slug — passes. Just a spec typo in the request.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 670 lines — approaching size threshold; consider splitting into routers/ (auth, public, admin, uploads) + services/pdf.py for maintainability.
Startup uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup') — migrate to FastAPI lifespan context manager.

PUT /api/admin/articles/{id} does not guard against slug collisions when a user changes slug to another existing article's slug — could break the unique index at write time with a 500. Add duplicate-slug check like POST does.
ObjectId(article_id) / ObjectId(job_id) can raise bson.errors.InvalidId returning 500 on malformed IDs — wrap and 400.
verify_password swallows all exceptions silently; ok for MVP but log at debug level.
No rate limiting on /api/auth/login (brute-force) or /api/contacts (spam). Playbook expects brute-force lockout after 5 fails — not implemented.
JWT stored client-side only via bearer header — playbook mentions httpOnly cookies + CORS allow_credentials; current setup uses bearer + wildcard CORS. Fine for admin panel but note the deviation.
GET /api/uploads/resume/{fid} is public — any file uploaded is retrievable by UUID without auth. Consider gating behind admin token or signed URLs.
seed_data_creation: None — relied on startup seed (admin user, 5 jobs, 3 articles, 4 research posts). Test-created articles/jobs are cleaned up in the CRUD test. Test-prefixed contacts/leads/applications remain in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. Reusable pytest file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from env. Admin creds live in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. If admin CRUD tests are run repeatedly, they self-clean their 'test-article-crud-2026' article and TEST job. No frontend tests written yet.
